all_deltas.content:
acti-006: IF a tooling call to find a user by name+zip fails (e.g., no match) → THEN ask the user for their email address and call find_user_id_by_email(email). IF find_user_id_by_email also fails, inform the user and offer troubleshooting (re-check email/name/zip) or escalation to a human agent.

ADDITION (symmetry for email lookups): IF a tooling call to find a user by email fails (e.g., 'User not found') → THEN ask the user to confirm the email and/or provide their name and zip code and call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip). IF find_user_id_by_name_zip also fails, inform the user and offer troubleshooting (re-check email/name/zip), try alternative emails the user provides, or offer escalation to a human agent. If either lookup eventually returns a user_id, proceed with get_user_details(user_id) as usual.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show find_user_id_by_email can fail and agents successfully recovered by asking for alternate email or name+zip; playbook lacked explicit guidance for the reverse fallback (email -> name+zip). Making the lookup guidance symmetric prevents confusion and ensures a clear next step when email lookup fails.

all_deltas.content: conf-005b: IF a user asks for tracking information or a tracking link → THEN provide the tracking ID(s) exactly as returned in get_order_details and note the carrier if available. If the tooling or system can generate a carrier tracking URL, include that direct link. If not, tell the user they can paste the tracking ID into the carrier's tracking page (and optionally provide the carrier's website) and offer to escalate or contact the carrier on their behalf if tracking appears inconsistent or the package shows delivered but wasn't received.
all_deltas.rationale: Users requested a tracking link and the agent responded with the tracking ID but there is no explicit playbook rule describing how to present tracking info or when to generate/provide links. Adding this clarifies expected behavior and user-facing guidance.

all_deltas.content: IF the user provides multiple potential identifiers (for example, more than one email address or multiple name+zip combinations) → THEN attempt lookups sequentially in the order the user provided them until a user_id is returned. If a lookup succeeds, proceed with get_user_details(user_id). If all provided identifiers fail to return a user_id, inform the user which identifiers were not found, ask the user to confirm or correct their identifiers (or provide a different identifier), and offer escalation to a human agent if they cannot provide a valid identifier.
all_deltas.rationale: Users sometimes supply multiple candidate identifiers (e.g., two emails). Tooling should try each in turn and then prompt/offer escalation if none match instead of failing on the first attempt.

all_deltas.content:
acti-005: IF calling modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or any other tooling call that modifies an order's state → THEN verify every item_id listed to be replaced, exchanged, or returned is present in the most recently retrieved order details for that order_id. If any provided item_id is not found in the current cached order state, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and reconcile the order contents, inform the user of the mismatch, and ask the user to confirm the correct item_ids (or which order they intended) before making the tooling call. Do not execute any tooling call with invalid or out-of-order item_ids.

Also verify the order's current status is appropriate for the requested tooling call (for modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address the order must be 'pending'; for exchange_delivered_order_items and return_delivered_order_items the order must be 'delivered'). If you do not have up-to-date order details or the cached state may be stale, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before proceeding. For actions that propose replacement item_ids (e.g., modify_pending_order_items or exchanges), verify each proposed new_item_id exists, is available, and is different from the current item_id (for example by calling get_product_details(product_id) or otherwise validating variant availability and item_id existence). Verify that counts and any replacement rules required by the tooling are satisfied (for example, equal counts when required). If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before attempting any further actions on that same order in the session. Use the refreshed order details to verify the validity and allowable state for subsequent actions and to avoid issuing tooling calls that conflict with the updated order state.
all_deltas.rationale: Observed a workflow where modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address were called consecutively without an intermediate get_order_details refresh. Reinforcing that modify_pending_order_address is also an order-modifying call and must follow the same immediate-refresh requirement prevents state mismatches and invalid subsequent tooling calls.

all_deltas.content: IF the requested action depends on order state → THEN only perform actions appropriate for that state. Specifically: (a) pending orders: allow modifications or cancellations (subject to modify_pending_order_items rules); (b) delivered orders: allow returns or exchanges; (c) processed orders: treat as non-modifiable/non-cancellable until delivery (offer to initiate exchanges once the items are delivered and do not attempt modify/cancel tooling calls while status is 'processed'); (d) cancelled orders: do not proceed with modifications. If an order is in a state not explicitly supported for the requested action, inform the user and offer appropriate alternatives (for example, schedule or prepare an exchange to be performed after delivery, add a reminder, or escalate to a human agent if urgent).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show orders with status 'processed' that are not modifiable; the playbook did not explicitly cover 'processed' state. Clarifying that 'processed' is non-modifiable avoids attempting invalid tooling calls and sets user expectations.

all_deltas.content: acti-021: IF a tooling call to modify/exchange/return fails with an 'Insufficient gift card balance' (or similar payment/funding) error → THEN: (a) immediately call get_user_details(user_id) to refresh available on-file payment methods and the current gift card balance; (b) inform the user the gift card balance is insufficient and present the exact balances and the calculated remaining amount required for the exchange/price difference; (c) present clear options and obtain explicit confirmation before retrying: (1) apply the available gift card balance and charge the remaining amount to a specific alternate on-file payment method (show type and identifiable label) if the tooling supports mixed payments, (2) pay the full remaining amount with a specified on-file payment method, (3) add a new payment method to cover the remaining amount, or (4) cancel/adjust the request; (d) if the tooling supports mixed-payment flows, include the correct payment_method_id(s) in the retry call; (e) if the tooling does NOT support mixing a gift card with another payment method, explicitly inform the user of that limitation and offer the alternatives in (c) (add a payment method, pay full amount with an alternate method, or cancel/adjust), and obtain explicit user confirmation before proceeding; (f) if the user requests an option that tooling cannot implement or if repeated verified payment info still results in an identical tooling error, offer escalation to a human agent (transfer_to_human_agents with a short summary) rather than repeatedly retrying the same tooling call.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectory 5 demonstrates an 'Insufficient gift card balance' error during an exchange where only a gift card existed on file. The playbook lacked explicit guidance for partially applying gift-card balances or for presenting and obtaining confirmation of alternatives when the gift card is insufficient. This rule fills that gap and prevents blind retries while ensuring the user is informed and in control.

all_deltas.content: IF the user requests an exchange/modification, asks for tracking, requests a return, or otherwise asks for an order-specific action but does NOT provide a specific order_id or item_id → THEN search the user's recent orders (by calling get_user_details(user_id) to obtain the user's order list and calling get_order_details(order_id) for each relevant order) for items matching the user's described product or description. If a matching item is found, identify the order and explicitly confirm with the user (provide order_id, item name, item_id, and key options) before presenting replacement options, providing tracking numbers, or performing any action. If multiple orders/items match the description, present the matches and ask the user to confirm which specific order/item they mean. If no matching item is found in the user's orders, do NOT assume the item exists in an order; instead ask the user to (a) specify which order they meant or provide the order_id or (b) provide the exact item_id/item name/options they want acted on before proceeding. Only after confirming the correct order/item should you proceed with order-specific tooling calls.
all_deltas.rationale: In Conversation 1 the agent provided a tracking number and initiated a return for an order that did not contain the described product (tablet). The system must require searching all of the user's orders for the described item and confirming the correct order/item with the user before supplying tracking or performing returns/exchanges.

all_deltas.content: acti-010 (REVISED): IF the user requests to update their account shipping address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the default address. IF one or more pending orders also need their shipping address changed → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each relevant pending order and confirm the updated address and any effects on order price or delivery. ADDITIONAL: If the user asks to use/copy the shipping address from a specific past order (for example: “use the address where my recent tablet was delivered”) or references an order rather than providing an address, first call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve that order’s shipping address. Present the full retrieved address to the user and obtain explicit confirmation before calling modify_user_address or modify_pending_order_address. If the user did not provide a specific order_id, search recent orders (via get_user_details and get_order_details) for the referenced delivery and ask the user to confirm which order/address to use. Do not update account or pending-order addresses without the user’s explicit confirmation of the exact address to be written to the account or order.
all_deltas.rationale: A user may ask to copy an address from a past order without giving the address explicitly; the system should retrieve and show the exact address and get explicit confirmation before making account/order changes (observed in Conversation 4).

all_deltas.content: acti-0XX: IF the user requests to swap or transfer items between different orders (for example, move an item from a delivered order to replace an item in a pending order, or use an item from one order as the replacement for another order) → THEN do NOT attempt to transfer item ownership or directly move item_ids between orders. Instead: (a) call get_order_details for each involved order to retrieve the current state and item details; (b) explain to the user that items cannot be moved across orders directly and present the supported alternatives: initiate an exchange or return on the order that currently holds the item being returned/exchanged, and separately modify, cancel, or place a new order for the desired replacement item on the other order; (c) if the user asks to replace an item in a pending order with a variant that exists only as an item_id in another order, verify availability of that variant via get_product_details/list_all_product_types and present available replacement variants (do not assume inter-order transfers); (d) if tooling prevents accomplishing the user’s requested cross-order outcome, offer escalation to a human agent via transfer_to_human_agents with a short summary. Do not attempt cross-order item transfers or rely on inter-order item_ids as valid replacements without explicit tooling support.
all_deltas.rationale: Users sometimes request cross-order swaps (observed in Conversation 2). Tooling does not support moving items directly between orders; the agent must instead treat each order separately, verify availability, offer alternatives, or escalate.

all_deltas.content:
acti-005: IF calling modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or any other tooling call that modifies an order's state → THEN verify every item_id listed to be replaced, exchanged, or returned is present in the most recently retrieved order details for that order_id. If any provided item_id is not found in the current cached order state, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and reconcile the order contents, inform the user of the mismatch, and ask the user to confirm the correct item_ids (or which order they intended) before making the tooling call. Do not execute any tooling call with invalid or out-of-order item_ids.

Also verify the order's current status is appropriate for the requested tooling call (for modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address the order must be 'pending'; for exchange_delivered_order_items and return_delivered_order_items the order must be 'delivered'). IF you do not have up-to-date order details or the cached state may be stale, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before proceeding. For actions that propose replacement item_ids (e.g., modify_pending_order_items or exchanges), verify each proposed new_item_id exists, is available, and is different from the current item_id (for example by calling get_product_details(product_id) or otherwise validating variant availability and item_id existence). Verify that counts and any replacement rules required by the tooling are satisfied (for example, equal counts when required). If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before attempting any further actions on that same order in the session. Present the refreshed order summary to the user (including updated items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history) and explicitly confirm with the user that they want to proceed with any additional changes. Before attempting any further modifications that change payment_method_id or payment allocation for that order, retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) and obtain explicit confirmation of which payment method to use (see acti-015). If get_order_details reveals the order is no longer in a modifiable state, inform the user immediately, explain the limitation, and offer alternatives (for example: place a new order, escalate to a human agent, or accept the current state). Do not retry modification tooling calls without a refreshed order state and explicit user confirmation of any changed parameters (including payment method).
all_deltas.rationale: Agents attempted further changes (including switching payment methods) without refreshing the order state and without presenting available payment methods; a stronger requirement to refresh and present the updated order and to obtain explicit confirmation (especially for payment changes) will prevent failed retries like in Conversation 5.

all_deltas.content: acti-023: IF modify_pending_order_items cannot remove a specific item (for example because replacements must be 1:1 or 'Variant not found') and the user requests removal of one or more items from a pending order → THEN: (a) explain clearly that modify_pending_order_items cannot remove items without a same-count replacement and list the supported alternatives (cancel the entire order, leave the order as-is, or place a separate new order for the desired items); (b) if the user indicates they prefer cancelling the entire order as the chosen alternative, explicitly confirm with the user that cancelling will cancel every item in the order and trigger a full-order refund per system policy (state the refund destination and any relevant timing expectations); (c) offer to place a new order for the remaining/desired items on the user's behalf or guide the user through placing a new order if they prefer to preserve some items; (d) obtain an explicit, unambiguous confirmation from the user that they want the entire order cancelled (not just the single item) before calling cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). If the user does not provide explicit confirmation of whole-order cancellation, do not call cancel_pending_order; instead proceed with the selected alternative (new order, keep existing order, or escalate).
all_deltas.rationale: An agent canceled an entire pending order after a failed item-level replacement despite the user asking to remove a single item and add a different one; the playbook must require an explicit confirmation that the user understands cancel_pending_order will cancel the whole order and offer the option to place a new order for replacements to avoid unintended full-order cancellations (observed in Conversation 4).

all_deltas.content: IF the order status is 'pending' and the user requests to change/replace a pending item → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: If the proposed replacements would result in any price difference (the new items cost more or less than the items being replaced), do NOT call modify_pending_order_items until you have: (a) computed the price difference (by comparing current order item prices and the new variant prices, using get_product_details or other tooling as needed), (b) presented the exact price difference to the user (showing whether it is an additional charge or a refund, the amount, and how it will be applied), (c) retrieved and presented the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) if the user has not already specified a payment_method_id and indicated which methods the tooling supports for the requested action, and (d) obtained the user's explicit confirmation of the exact payment method to use and explicit agreement to proceed with the computed charge/refund. If the original payment was a gift card, follow prec-001 and acti-013 regarding refund destinations and make this clear to the user before proceeding. Only after the user confirms the payment method and the price-difference handling should you call modify_pending_order_items. If the user does not confirm, offer alternatives (place a new order, cancel the pending order, or adjust the requested replacements).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed modify_pending_order_items being executed and an extra charge applied (Conversation 2) without the assistant presenting payment methods and obtaining explicit confirmation of the payment method and price difference beforehand. The playbook previously required payment confirmation in some contexts but did not explicitly mandate it prior to calling modify_pending_order_items when a price difference exists.

all_deltas.content: IF the user requests that refunds/price differences be applied to 'another on-file payment method' but does not specify which one → THEN first retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (prefer non-gift-card methods when the original payment was not a gift card) including identifiable labels (type and last four digits or other label) and indicate which methods the tooling supports for the requested action. Additionally, when a requested action will create a price difference (either a charge or a refund), explicitly show the calculated price difference amount and clearly state whether the user will be charged or refunded, and which on-file payment method (type and last four or other identifiable label) will be used for that charge or refund. Prompt the user to explicitly select and confirm which on-file payment method to use and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact amount and payment method before proceeding. Only after the user confirms a specific payment method and the exact price difference, proceed with the order-changing action using that payment_method_id. If the original payment was a gift card, prefer non‑gift-card methods only when supported by tooling and policy; if tooling does not support changing the refund/payment destination from the original gift card, inform the user and obtain explicit confirmation to proceed with the system behavior. IF no suitable alternate payment method is available, inform the user and offer alternatives (for example: add a new payment method, use a different on-file method they confirm, initiate a return flow if appropriate, or escalate to a human agent). BEFORE executing any irreversible order-changing action, inform the user which on-file payment method (type and last four digits or other identifiable label) will be used and obtain explicit confirmation. IF the user does not confirm, prompt the user to specify which payment method to use or fall back to refunding to the original payment method per policy.
all_deltas.rationale: Evidence (Conversation 2) showed a pending-order modification that resulted in an additional charge being applied without the assistant explicitly presenting the calculated price difference and obtaining confirmation of the payment method beforehand. Strengthening acti-015 to require presentation of the exact amount (charge or refund) and explicit confirmation closes this gap.

all_deltas.content:
acti-005: IF calling modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or any other tooling call that modifies an order's state → THEN verify every item_id listed to be replaced, exchanged, or returned is present in the most recently retrieved order details for that order_id. If any provided item_id is not found in the current cached order state, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and reconcile the order contents, inform the user of the mismatch, and ask the user to confirm the correct item_ids (or which order they intended) before making the tooling call. Do not execute any tooling call with invalid or out-of-order item_ids.

Also verify the order's current status is appropriate for the requested tooling call (for modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address the order must be 'pending'; for exchange_delivered_order_items and return_delivered_order_items the order must be 'delivered'). IF you do not have up-to-date order details or the cached state may be stale, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before proceeding. For actions that propose replacement item_ids (e.g., modify_pending_order_items or exchanges), verify each proposed new_item_id exists, is available, and is different from the current item_id (for example by calling get_product_details(product_id) or otherwise validating variant availability and item_id existence). Verify that counts and any replacement rules required by the tooling are satisfied (for example, equal counts when required).

When a user requests exchanging or modifying multiple quantities of the same product, explicitly verify that the order contains that many distinct item_ids (each unit must be represented by an actual item_id in the order). Do NOT assume that repeating a single item_id multiple times is valid. If the requested quantity exceeds the number of matching item_ids in the referenced order, or if the user references multiple units that may span multiple orders, ask the user to (a) specify the exact item_id(s) and/or order_id(s) to act on, or (b) confirm you should search their recent orders for matching item_ids. If items span multiple orders, do not combine item_ids from different orders into a single tooling call; instead follow the multi-order exchange handling rules (perform per-order verification and per-order tooling calls).

If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before attempting any further actions on that same order in the session. Present the refreshed order summary to the user (including updated items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history) and explicitly confirm with the user that they want to proceed with any additional changes. Before attempting any further modifications that change payment_method_id or payment allocation for that order, retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) and obtain explicit confirmation of which payment method to use (see acti-015). If get_order_details reveals the order is no longer in a modifiable state, inform the user immediately, explain the limitation, and offer alternatives (for example: place a new order, escalate to a human agent, or accept the current state). Do not retry modification tooling calls without a refreshed order state and explicit user confirmation of any changed parameters (including payment method).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed a failed exchange attempt when the assistant attempted to reference the same item_id multiple times and/or combine items across orders without confirming distinct item_ids and per-order ownership. The rule must explicitly require verifying distinct item_ids for multiple-quantity requests and clarifying that a single item_id cannot be repeated to represent multiple units.

all_deltas.content: acti-0XX (multi-order exchanges): IF a user requests exchanges (or similar delivered-item modifications) that involve items located in more than one order → THEN treat each order as a separate operation: (a) call get_order_details(order_id) for each implicated order to verify the item_ids, order status, and payment history; (b) compute and present per-order price differences (refunds or charges) and an aggregated net total to the user, clearly indicating which amount applies to which order and which payment method will be used for each adjustment; (c) obtain explicit confirmation from the user for each order's exchange (including confirmation of the exact item_ids, the replacement new_item_ids, and the payment method to use for any charge/refund); (d) execute separate exchange_delivered_order_items calls (one per order) using only item_ids that belong to that specific order; (e) after each successful tooling call, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and present the updated order state and next steps; (f) if any per-order call fails (counts mismatch, item not found, payment error), report the specific failure, present corrective options for that particular order, and do not retry other order calls until the user confirms how to proceed. If performing multiple per-order actions, do not batch item_ids from different orders into a single tooling call.
all_deltas.rationale: A trajectory attempted to exchange two identical laptops by combining items into a single call and repeating an item_id, causing tooling errors. The system must require per-order verification, per-order tooling calls, and explicit user confirmation for multi-order exchanges to avoid count/matching and ownership errors.

all_deltas.content:
acti-005: IF calling modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or any other tooling call that modifies an order's state → THEN verify every item_id listed to be replaced, exchanged, or returned is present in the most recently retrieved order details for that order_id. If any provided item_id is not found in the current cached order state, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and reconcile the order contents, inform the user of the mismatch, and ask the user to confirm the correct item_ids (or which order they intended) before making the tooling call. Do not execute any tooling call with invalid or out-of-order item_ids.

Also verify the order's current status is appropriate for the requested tooling call (for modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address the order must be 'pending'; for exchange_delivered_order_items and return_delivered_order_items the order must be 'delivered'). IF you do not have up-to-date order details or the cached state may be stale, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state before proceeding. For actions that propose replacement item_ids (e.g., modify_pending_order_items or exchanges), verify each proposed new_item_id exists, is available, and is different from the current item_id (for example by calling get_product_details(product_id) or otherwise validating variant availability and item_id existence). Verify that counts and any replacement rules required by the tooling are satisfied (for example, equal counts when required).

When a user requests exchanging or modifying multiple quantities of the same product, explicitly verify that the order contains that many distinct item_ids (each unit must be represented by an actual item_id in the order). Do NOT assume that repeating a single item_id multiple times is valid. If the requested quantity exceeds the number of matching item_ids in the referenced order, or if the user references multiple units that may span multiple orders, ask the user to (a) specify the exact item_id(s) and/or order_id(s) to act on, or (b) confirm you should search their recent orders for matching item_ids. If items span multiple orders, do not combine item_ids from different orders into a single tooling call; instead follow the multi-order exchange handling rules (perform per-order verification and per-order tooling calls).

If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state BEFORE attempting any further actions on that same order in the session and present the refreshed order summary to the user (including updated items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history). EXCEPTION: If the modifying tooling call's response itself includes the full, authoritative, up-to-date order details (items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history) returned by the system, a separate immediate get_order_details(order_id) call is optional. In that case, the assistant must verify the tooling response includes complete order details and may use that response to present the refreshed order summary. If the tooling response does not include full order details or its completeness is uncertain, the assistant MUST call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh state before further actions. Before attempting any further modifications that change payment_method_id or payment allocation for that order, retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) and obtain explicit confirmation of which payment method to use (see acti-015). If get_order_details reveals the order is no longer in a modifiable state, inform the user immediately, explain the limitation, and offer alternatives (for example: place a new order, escalate to a human agent, or accept the current state). Do not retry modification tooling calls without a refreshed order state and explicit user confirmation of any changed parameters (including payment method).
all_deltas.rationale: Tooling responses in multiple trajectories returned full updated order details and agents relied on those responses without making a separate get_order_details call. Allowing an explicit exception (when the tooling already returns full, authoritative order details) avoids unnecessary duplicate calls while preserving the requirement to refresh state when the response is incomplete or uncertain.

all_deltas.content: IF the user provides an ordered list of preferred variant attributes or fallback preferences (for example: 'I want 8 bar; if not available, use 9 bar; otherwise 7 bar') → THEN evaluate variant availability sequentially in the user's specified priority order. Present the first available variant that satisfies the highest-priority available preference (including item_id, options, availability, and price) and explicitly request the user's confirmation before initiating any exchange or replacement. If none of the listed preferences are available, clearly inform the user, present the closest available alternatives (with availability and prices), and ask whether to proceed with one of those alternatives, to be notified when a preferred variant becomes available, or to cancel the request.
all_deltas.rationale: Users sometimes provide prioritized fallback preferences for variants. The assistant should check availability in the order the user specified and present the first available match for explicit confirmation, rather than choosing an alternate arbitrarily.

all_deltas.content: IF the user has not provided identification (name+zip or email) → THEN do not proceed with account- or order-specific lookups or actions, except when the user provides a specific order_id or a valid account user_id. IF a valid order_id is provided by the user, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve order and associated user information and proceed as appropriate. IF a valid account user_id is provided by the user, call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account information and proceed as appropriate. If ownership cannot be confirmed from the order details or the user disputes the order, ask for additional identification or offer escalation.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users supplying an internal account user_id (e.g., 'mia_garcia_4516') and the assistant proceeding by calling get_user_details. The precondition rule should explicitly allow user_id as valid identification.

all_deltas.content: IF the user provides an account user_id (for example, a username-like ID such as 'mia_garcia_4516' or similar) → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain the user's account details and proceed with order- or account-specific actions per existing lookup and verification rules. If the provided user_id is not found, inform the user and ask for an alternate identifier (email or name+zip) or offer escalation.
all_deltas.rationale: Conversation evidence shows users sometimes provide an account user_id directly; the assistant should treat this as a valid identifier and call get_user_details, with fallback behavior if lookup fails.

all_deltas.content: IF the user provides an order identifier that does not match the system's expected formatting (for example missing a prefix like 'W' or '#' in front of a numeric id) → THEN attempt common normalized variants before asking the user to re-confirm (for example try '#<ID>', 'W<ID>', and '#W<ID>') by calling get_order_details for each likely variant. If none of the normalized attempts succeed, inform the user which variants were tried, state that no matching order was found, and ask the user to confirm or provide the exact order_id (or provide alternate identification) before proceeding.
all_deltas.rationale: Users frequently omit or mistype order prefixes (e.g., giving '9502126' instead of 'W9502126'); the assistant should attempt common normalized forms to find the correct order before asking the user to re-enter it.

all_deltas.content: IF a return/exchange action will result in follow-up instructions or return labels being sent by email AND the user indicates they do not have an email address or cannot access their email, THEN before calling return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items: (a) check the user's account for an email on file via get_user_details(user_id); (b) explicitly confirm with the user that it is acceptable to send return/exchange instructions to the on-file email address if one exists; (c) if the user does not consent to using the on-file email or no valid email exists, offer alternatives: (1) escalate to a human agent to arrange an alternative delivery method for the instructions (call transfer_to_human_agents with a short summary), or (2) proceed with initiating the return/exchange only after informing the user that system-generated return instructions will be emailed and that they must contact customer service to arrange an alternative if needed; (d) if the user prefers the assistant to proceed and accept that instructions will be emailed to the on-file email, record the user's consent and proceed with the tooling call using the appropriate payment_method_id; (e) do not assume consent to use the account email when the user explicitly says they do not have or cannot access email.
all_deltas.rationale: In Conversation 1 the user said they did not have an email but the assistant initiated a return and stated instructions would be emailed without explicitly confirming use of the account email. The playbook lacks a clear requirement to confirm use of an on-file email or to escalate/offer alternatives when the user cannot receive email.

all_deltas.content: IF the user asks about product options, variants, availability, or requests an exchange → call list_all_product_types() if you need to map a product type to a product_id, then call get_product_details(product_id) to list variants, availability, and prices. When presenting product variants or exchange candidates, explicitly surface each variant’s "available" status and present only variants with available=true as immediate, actionable replacements. Before asserting a variant as the "cheapest available" or otherwise recommending it, verify that the variant's available flag is true in the most recent get_product_details response; do not promote or present variants flagged available=false as available alternatives. If a variant is unavailable, label it clearly as "unavailable" or "backordered" and offer it only if the user is willing to wait or wants to be notified when it becomes available. If the requested product type is not present in list_all_product_types(), ask the user for a more specific product name, product code/ID, or additional identifying details (brand, intended use, or a similar product name) before proceeding; if the user cannot provide more details, offer to broaden the search to related categories or escalate to a human agent. If a product category is found but no suitable variants match the user’s requested attributes, inform the user the exact option isn’t available, present closely related alternatives with their availability and prices, and confirm whether to proceed with an alternative or escalate.
all_deltas.rationale: Agents in the trajectories presented variants as "cheapest available" even when tooling marked those variants unavailable. The rule must explicitly require verifying the 'available' flag before recommending variants and labeling unavailable variants clearly.

all_deltas.content: IF the user indicates they have just added or updated a payment method during the session → THEN immediately call get_user_details(user_id) to refresh the account's saved payment methods. If the newly added payment method does not appear in the refreshed account details, inform the user that the system does not yet reflect the change and advise common troubleshooting steps (refresh the page, log out and log back in, wait a short time for propagation, or re-add the payment method). Offer to wait and retry the refresh while the user confirms the addition, guide them through adding the method in the web/app UI, or escalate to a human agent if the method never appears. Do not assume the new payment method exists or proceed with payment actions until the payment method is present in get_user_details and the user explicitly confirms which method to use.
all_deltas.rationale: In trajectories, a user added a new card but the system did not show it; the agent needed to re-check account data and provide specific guidance. Adding an explicit refresh-and-troubleshoot step prevents proceeding with payments using non-present methods and aligns agent behavior with observed tooling propagation delays.

all_deltas.content: IF the order status is 'delivered' and the user requests to either exchange or return delivered items → THEN for an exchange call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id); for a return call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id). ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: If the proposed exchange will result in any price difference (the new items cost more or less than the items being exchanged), do NOT call exchange_delivered_order_items until you have: (a) computed the exact price difference (using get_product_details or other tooling as needed), (b) presented the exact price difference to the user (showing whether it is an additional charge or a refund, the amount, and how it will be applied), (c) retrieved and presented the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) if the user has not already specified a payment_method_id and indicated which methods the tooling supports for the requested action, and (d) obtained the user's explicit confirmation of the exact payment method to use and explicit agreement to proceed with the computed charge/refund. If the user does not confirm, offer alternatives (initiate a return, choose a different replacement, or escalate to a human agent). After performing an exchange_delivered_order_items call that modifies payment allocation or records an exchange_price_difference, follow acti-005: verify items and order state, and refresh order details (call get_order_details(order_id)) unless the tooling response includes full, authoritative order details.
all_deltas.rationale: Delivered-item exchanges in the trajectories were executed without first presenting and confirming payment-method selection and the exact price difference. The playbook already requires this for pending-order modifications, so require the same explicit price-difference and payment-method confirmation for delivered exchanges to avoid unexpected charges/refunds.

all_deltas.content:
acti-005 (updated): IF calling modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, return_delivered_order_items, cancel_pending_order, or any other tooling call that modifies an order's state → THEN verify every item_id listed to be replaced, exchanged, or returned is present in the most recently retrieved order details for that order_id. If any provided item_id is not found in the current cached order state, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh and reconcile the order contents, inform the user of the mismatch, and ask the user to confirm the correct item_ids (or which order they intended) before making the tooling call. Do not execute any tooling call with invalid or out-of-order item_ids.

Also verify the order's current status is appropriate for the requested tooling call (for modify_pending_order_items and modify_pending_order_address the order must be 'pending'; for exchange_delivered_order_items and return_delivered_order_items the order must be 'delivered'). IF the order is already in an active return/exchange state (for example 'return requested' or 'exchange requested'), do NOT assume you can append additional items by re-calling return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items. Instead: (a) call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the current return/exchange activity and which item_ids are already included, (b) check whether the tooling explicitly supports adding items to an existing return/exchange request before attempting the call, and (c) if the tooling does NOT support modifying an existing return/exchange, inform the user that additional items cannot be appended to the current request and present alternatives (initiate a separate return/exchange for the additional items, cancel and re-create the return/exchange if appropriate, or escalate to a human agent to request a manual merge/adjustment). Obtain explicit user confirmation of which alternative to proceed with before taking any further action. Do not repeatedly retry adding items without tooling confirmation or explicit user confirmation of the chosen alternative.

When a user requests exchanging or modifying multiple quantities of the same product, explicitly verify that the order contains that many distinct item_ids (each unit must be represented by an actual item_id in the order). Do NOT assume that repeating a single item_id multiple times is valid. If the requested quantity exceeds the number of matching item_ids in the referenced order, or if the user references multiple units that may span multiple orders, ask the user to (a) specify the exact item_id(s) and/or order_id(s) to act on, or (b) confirm you should search their recent orders for matching item_ids. If items span multiple orders, do not combine item_ids from different orders into a single tooling call; instead follow the multi-order exchange handling rules (perform per-order verification and per-order tooling calls).

If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state BEFORE attempting any further actions on that same order in the session and present the refreshed order summary to the user (including updated items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history). EXCEPTION: If the modifying tooling call's response itself includes the full, authoritative, up-to-date order details (items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history) returned by the system, a separate immediate get_order_details(order_id) call is optional. In that case, the assistant must verify the tooling response includes complete order details and may use that response to present the refreshed order summary. If the tooling response does not include full order details or its completeness is uncertain, the assistant MUST call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh state before further actions. Before attempting any further modifications that change payment_method_id or payment allocation for that order, retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) and obtain explicit confirmation of which payment method to use (see acti-015). If get_order_details reveals the order is no longer in a modifiable state, inform the user immediately, explain the limitation, and offer alternatives (for example: place a new order, escalate to a human agent, or accept the current state). Do not retry modification tooling calls without a refreshed order state and explicit user confirmation of any changed parameters (including payment method).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed an attempt to add items to an order already marked 'return requested' which failed; the playbook lacked explicit instructions to check whether tooling supports appending items to existing return/exchange requests and to present alternatives when not supported.

all_deltas.content: IF a user requests adding additional delivered items to an order that already has an active return or exchange request (for example order.status is 'return requested' or 'exchange requested') → THEN: (a) call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm which items are already included in the existing request and the current order state; (b) verify whether the tooling supports modifying an existing return/exchange to append additional item_ids. If the tooling supports it, present the exact additions, any price differences or shipping/logistics implications, and obtain explicit user confirmation before calling the tooling. If the tooling does NOT support adding items to an existing request, inform the user clearly that the system cannot append items to the current return/exchange and present alternatives: (1) initiate a separate return/exchange for the additional items, (2) escalate to a human agent to request merging or manual adjustment (call transfer_to_human_agents with a short summary including order_id and the attempted modification), or (3) advise contacting customer service. Obtain explicit user confirmation of the selected alternative before proceeding. Do not repeatedly retry the same add-to-return tooling call without tooling confirmation and explicit user consent.
all_deltas.rationale: Observed failure when attempting to add an item to an order already in 'return requested' state; the playbook did not specify how to handle adding items to an existing return/exchange request or which alternatives to offer.

all_deltas.content: acti-001 (updated): IF the order status is 'pending' and the user requests to change/replace a pending item → THEN call modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: If the proposed replacements would result in any price difference (the new items cost more or less than the items being replaced), do NOT call modify_pending_order_items until you have: (a) computed the price difference (by comparing current order item prices and the new variant prices, using get_product_details or other tooling as needed), (b) presented the exact price difference to the user (showing whether it is an additional charge or a refund, the amount, and how it will be applied), (c) retrieved and presented the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) if the user has not already specified a payment_method_id and indicated which methods the tooling supports for the requested action, and (d) obtained the user's explicit confirmation of the exact payment method to use and explicit agreement to proceed with the computed charge/refund. EXCEPTION: If the assistant has already shown the replacement variant's current price(s) to the user and the user then gives an explicit, unambiguous instruction to proceed (for example, 'Yes, go ahead and make the change' or equivalent) AND there is exactly one eligible on-file payment method available for the required adjustment, the assistant MAY proceed to call modify_pending_order_items using that single on-file payment_method_id without a separate additional selection step. In that case the assistant MUST: (1) immediately state the exact computed price difference and which on-file payment method (type and last four or other identifiable label) will be used in the same message confirming the action, (2) record the user's affirmative instruction as the consent to use that payment method and the computed charge/refund, and (3) follow all other verification requirements in acti-005 (verify item_ids, order state). If the original payment was a gift card, follow prec-001 and acti-013 regarding refund destinations and make this clear to the user before proceeding. Only after the user confirms the payment method and the price-difference handling should you call modify_pending_order_items. If the user does not confirm, offer alternatives (place a new order, cancel the pending order, or adjust the requested replacements).
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents proceeding with modify_pending_order_items after the user explicitly said 'go ahead' and after being shown replacement prices while only one on-file payment method existed. The playbook required a separate explicit payment-method confirmation in all cases; adding a narrow exception prevents unnecessary extra prompts while preserving accountability and required disclosures.

all_deltas.content: acti-002 (updated): IF the order status is 'delivered' and the user requests to either exchange or return delivered items → THEN for an exchange call exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id); for a return call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id). ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT: If the proposed exchange will result in any price difference (the new items cost more or less than the items being exchanged), do NOT call exchange_delivered_order_items until you have: (a) computed the exact price difference (using get_product_details or other tooling as needed), (b) presented the exact price difference to the user (showing whether it is an additional charge or a refund, the amount, and how it will be applied), (c) retrieved and presented the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) if the user has not already specified a payment_method_id and indicated which methods the tooling supports for the requested action, and (d) obtained the user's explicit confirmation of the exact payment method to use and explicit agreement to proceed with the computed charge/refund. EXCEPTION: If the assistant has already shown the replacement variant's current price(s) to the user and the user then gives an explicit, unambiguous instruction to proceed (for example, 'Yes, go ahead and make the change' or equivalent) AND there is exactly one eligible on-file payment method available for the required adjustment, the assistant MAY proceed to call exchange_delivered_order_items using that single on-file payment_method_id without a separate additional selection step. In that case the assistant MUST: (1) immediately state the exact computed price difference and which on-file payment method (type and last four or other identifiable label) will be used in the same message confirming the action, (2) record the user's affirmative instruction as consent to use that payment method and the computed charge/refund, and (3) follow acti-005 verification steps after the tooling call (refresh order details, present refreshed order summary) unless the tooling response includes full authoritative order details. If the user does not confirm, offer alternatives (initiate a return, choose a different replacement, or escalate to a human agent). After performing an exchange_delivered_order_items call that modifies payment allocation or records an exchange_price_difference, follow acti-005: verify items and order state, and refresh order details (call get_order_details(order_id)) unless the tooling response includes full, authoritative order details.
all_deltas.rationale: Trajectories show exchanges being performed after the user explicitly approved the replacement variant and where only one on-file payment method existed; the playbook's strict requirement to re-present payment options in every case created unnecessary extra confirmation steps. This refinement preserves required disclosures while allowing a streamlined flow when user intent is clear and only one payment option exists.

all_deltas.content: acti-022 (updated): IF the user requests to swap or transfer items between different orders (for example, move an item from a delivered order to replace an item in a pending order, or use an item from one order as the replacement for another order) → THEN do NOT attempt to transfer item ownership or directly move item_ids between orders. Instead: (a) call get_order_details for each involved order to retrieve the current state and item details; (b) explain to the user that items cannot be moved across orders directly and present the supported alternatives: initiate an exchange or return on the order that currently holds the item being returned/exchanged, and separately modify, cancel, or place a new order for the desired replacement item on the other order; (c) if the user proposes using a replacement identified by an item_id that appears in another order, DO NOT assume that item_id is transferable. First verify the replacement by calling get_product_details(product_id) or list_all_product_types() to confirm whether that item_id corresponds to a product variant that is currently available (available=true). If get_product_details confirms the item_id is an available variant, the assistant may treat it as selecting that variant (not as transferring ownership) and proceed via the supported replace/modify/exchange tooling, but must explicitly inform the user that the assistant is selecting the product variant (not moving the physical item from another order). If the item_id is not found as an available variant, do NOT use an inter-order item_id as a replacement; instead present available replacement variants (with availability and price) and ask the user to confirm. (d) if tooling prevents accomplishing the user’s requested cross-order outcome, offer escalation to a human agent via transfer_to_human_agents with a short summary. Do not attempt cross-order item transfers or rely on inter-order item_ids as valid replacements without explicit tooling support and variant availability verification.
all_deltas.rationale: A trajectory used an item_id present in another order as the replacement without an explicit verification step. The playbook already discouraged cross-order transfers but needs a clearer required verification step: confirm via get_product_details that the item_id exists as an available variant before using it as a replacement and explicitly communicate that this is selecting the variant (not moving the physical item).

bullets.content: IF the user requests account- or order-specific assistance → THEN ask the user to provide either (a) name and zip code or (b) email address before proceeding.
IF the user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip) to obtain user_id.
IF the user provides email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email(email) to obtain user_id.
IF a user_id is obtained → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve user account info (email, payment methods, orders, address).
IF the user references a specific order or you will act on orders → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) for each relevant order_id to retrieve items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history.

IF the user requests cancellation of a pending order → THEN call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) and include the cancellation reason parameter. If the user provides a cancellation reason, use that reason. IF the user does not provide a reason, select and record a default valid cancellation reason (for example: 'no longer needed') and proceed with the cancellation, and inform the user which reason was recorded. REFINEMENT: If the user's intent is to remove or change one or more specific items within a pending order (rather than cancel the entire order), first attempt to fulfill the request with modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id). Do not call cancel_pending_order to remove a single item without first: (a) attempting modify_pending_order_items and (b) if modify_pending_order_items is not supported or fails, explaining the limitations and offering alternatives (modify with replacements, cancel entire order, or place a new order) and obtaining explicit confirmation from the user before cancelling the whole order. If the user explicitly confirms they want the entire order cancelled, proceed with cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason). ADDITIONAL REFINEMENT (refund destination): Before calling cancel_pending_order when the user requests a refund to a different payment method (for example, a gift card or another on-file method), explicitly: (1) confirm the user's desired refund destination and show the original payment method (type and last four if available), (2) verify whether the tooling supports specifying an alternate refund payment method for cancel_pending_order. If tooling does not support specifying a different refund destination, inform the user that cancellations will result in refunds to the original payment method per system behavior and get explicit confirmation to proceed. If the user still requests a refund to a different on-file method (or to a gift card) and the tooling cannot implement it, offer alternatives (for example: initiate a return so a store/gift-card refund can be applied where supported, or escalate to a human agent to request an exception). Do not proceed with cancel_pending_order under the assumption a different refund destination was used without either tooling confirmation or explicit user acknowledgement of the actual refund destination.

If items span multiple orders, do not combine item_ids from different orders into a single tooling call; instead follow the multi-order exchange handling rules (perform per-order verification and per-order tooling calls).

If any new_item_id is unavailable, identical to the current item, or would violate tooling count constraints, inform the user, present alternatives, and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact replacements before calling the tooling. Do not call modify_pending_order_items, modify_pending_order_address, exchange_delivered_order_items, or return_delivered_order_items if the order state does not match the action required.

After successfully performing any tooling call that modifies an order's state, immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state BEFORE attempting any further actions on that same order in the session and present the refreshed order summary to the user (including updated items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history). EXCEPTION: If the modifying tooling call's response itself includes the full, authoritative, up-to-date order details (items, item_ids, order status, and payment_history) returned by the system, a separate immediate get_order_details(order_id) call is optional. In that case, the assistant must verify the tooling response includes complete order details and may use that response to present the refreshed order summary. If the tooling response does not include full order details or its completeness is uncertain, the assistant MUST call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh state before further actions. Before attempting any further modifications that change payment_method_id or payment allocation for that order, retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (including type and identifiable label such as last four) and obtain explicit confirmation of which payment method to use (see acti-015). If get_order_details reveals the order is no longer in a modifiable state, inform the user immediately, explain the limitation, and offer alternatives (for example: place a new order, escalate to a human agent, or accept the current state). Do not retry modification tooling calls without a refreshed order state and explicit user confirmation of any changed parameters (including payment method).

acti-006: IF a tooling call to find a user by name+zip fails (e.g., no match) → THEN ask the user for their email address and call find_user_id_by_email(email). IF find_user_id_by_email also fails, inform the user and offer troubleshooting (re-check email/name/zip) or escalation to a human agent.

ADDITION (symmetry for email lookups): IF a tooling call to find a user by email fails (e.g., 'User not found') → THEN ask the user to confirm the email and/or provide their name and zip code and call find_user_id_by_name_zip(first_name, last_name, zip). IF find_user_id_by_name_zip also fails, inform the user and offer troubleshooting (re-check email/name/zip), try alternative emails the user provides, or offer escalation to a human agent. If either lookup eventually returns a user_id, proceed with get_user_details(user_id) as usual.

IF an exchange or modification is performed → THEN allow the system/tool to compute and record exchange_price_difference and record the exchange_payment_method_id in the order record (as observed in tool outputs).

IF a product has multiple variant attributes that affect the user's choice (e.g., backlight color, size, compatibility) → THEN ask the user which specific attribute value they prefer before proceeding.

IF about to perform an irreversible or order-changing action (modify, exchange, return, cancel) → THEN explicitly confirm the exact changes with the user (items to replace/return, target variants, and any noted prices) before executing the tooling call, and verify the current order state (e.g., delivered, pending, exchange_requested, return_requested) and any existing exchange/return activity for that order to ensure the requested action is valid.

IF presenting product options or an exchange candidate to the user → THEN communicate variant availability, key option attributes (e.g., compatibility, color), and the price for the option being proposed.

IF an action completes successfully → THEN tell the user the action was completed (e.g., 'Ok I made the change' / 'Done. Both orders have been cancelled') and ask if there is anything else you can help with. REFINEMENT: When the completed action involves a payment or refund, explicitly state the refund or charge details reported by the tooling (amount and the payment method used — type and last four digits or other identifiable label when available). If an exchange/modification results in a price difference, clearly communicate whether the user was charged or refunded and which payment method was used for that adjustment.

IF the user requests refunds to a different payment method than the original payment method used for that order → THEN by default refunds should go to the original payment method. If the original payment method was a gift card, refunds must be processed back to that gift card and may not be redirected to another on‑file payment method. Refunds to another on‑file payment method (e.g., different on‑file credit/debit card or on‑account gift card) may only be allowed when the original payment was not a gift card and when supported by the tooling. Refunds to external/unlinked payment methods not present on the account must be refused.

IF the user has not provided identification (name+zip or email) → THEN do not proceed with account- or order-specific lookups or actions, except when the user provides a specific order_id or a valid account user_id. IF a valid order_id is provided by the user, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve order and associated user information and proceed as appropriate. IF a valid account user_id is provided by the user, call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve account information and proceed as appropriate. If ownership cannot be confirmed from the order details or the user disputes the order, ask for additional identification or offer escalation.

IF the requested product variant does not exist or is unavailable → THEN inform the user that the exact option doesn't exist and offer available alternatives (e.g., different backlight, different size) before taking action.

IF the requested action depends on order state → THEN only perform actions appropriate for that state. Specifically: (a) pending orders: allow modifications or cancellations (subject to modify_pending_order_items rules); (b) delivered orders: allow returns or exchanges; (c) processed orders: treat as non-modifiable/non-cancellable until delivery (offer to initiate exchanges once the items are delivered and do not attempt modify/cancel tooling calls while status is 'processed'); (d) cancelled orders: do not proceed with modifications. If an order is in a state not explicitly supported for the requested action, inform the user and offer appropriate alternatives (for example, schedule or prepare an exchange to be performed after delivery, add a reminder, or escalate to a human agent if urgent).

IF cancelling a pending order via cancel_pending_order → THEN include a cancel_reason and expect the tool to record a refund to the original payment method as part of the cancellation flow (per observed tool outputs).

acti-008: IF a user requests escalation (explicitly asks for a human) or becomes abusive/angry and requests human assistance → THEN call transfer_to_human_agents({"summary": <short summary>}) to hand off. Include the user's most recent message as 'last_user_message' only if the tooling supports that parameter. If the tooling returns an error indicating an unexpected parameter (for example, 'unexpected keyword argument "last_user_message"'), retry the call with a payload containing only the 'summary' field. On successful transfer, mark the conversation as escalated. If escalation is requested after tooling failures that cannot be resolved, offer transfer to a human and proceed with transfer when the user agrees or is hostile.

acti-009: IF a tooling call to modify/exchange/return fails with an error indicating order state, non-delivered status, conflicting existing activity (e.g., 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged') OR with an error specific to payment/funding (for example, 'Insufficient gift card balance', 'card declined', or other payment-related failures) → THEN verify the current order state. If you do not have recent order details, call get_order_details(order_id) to refresh the order state. For payment/funding errors, call get_user_details(user_id) (or otherwise retrieve the user's available on-file payment methods) to determine which alternate payment methods exist and whether the tooling supports using them. Present the user with the available alternate on-file payment methods (showing type and identifiable label such as last four digits) and obtain explicit confirmation of which method to use before retrying the tooling call. If the tooling does not support specifying an alternate payment method or if no suitable alternate payment method exists, inform the user of that limitation, present alternatives (for example: add a new payment method, use a different on-file method, escalate to a human agent, or cancel/adjust the request), and obtain explicit instructions before proceeding. If you have already retrieved current order details in-session and the cached state is up-to-date, you may rely on those details to explain the reason for the failure to the user (including current order status, payment limitation, and any existing exchange/return flags) and offer corrective options (select a different payment method, adjust request, modify pending order, wait for completion, or escalate to a human). Do not repeatedly retry the same tooling call without state and payment-method verification and explicit user confirmation of a changed payment method.

acti-010 (REVISED): IF the user requests to update their account shipping address → THEN call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the default address. IF one or more pending orders also need their shipping address changed → THEN call modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each relevant pending order and confirm the updated address and any effects on order price or delivery. ADDITIONAL: If the user asks to use/copy the shipping address from a specific past order (for example: “use the address where my recent tablet was delivered”) or references an order rather than providing an address, first call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve that order’s shipping address. Present the full retrieved address to the user and obtain explicit confirmation before calling modify_user_address or modify_pending_order_address. If the user did not provide a specific order_id, search recent orders (via get_user_details and get_order_details) for the referenced delivery and ask the user to confirm which order/address to use. Do not update account or pending-order addresses without the user’s explicit confirmation of the exact address to be written to the account or order.

acti-011: IF a cancel_pending_order tooling call fails with an 'Invalid reason' error → THEN inform the user of the valid cancellation reasons (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake'), prompt the user to select one of those reasons, and retry cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) with the validated reason.

conf-005: IF the user is returning items that originate from different orders → THEN inform the user that each order's return is handled separately, that they will receive separate return instructions/emails for each order, and confirm whether they would like you to initiate returns for each order now.

IF the user requests to pay any price differences (for modifications/exchanges) using an on‑account gift card or another on‑file payment method → THEN allow the requested on‑file payment method to be used for the difference, provided the payment method has sufficient balance and is present on the user's account. Include the chosen payment_method_id in tooling calls for modify/exchange actions.

acti-012: IF a tooling call to exchange/return fails with an error indicating the order is not in a deliverable/delivered state (e.g., 'Non-delivered order cannot be exchanged' or 'Non-delivered order cannot be returned') → THEN immediately call get_order_details(order_id) to re‑verify the current order state. IF get_order_details confirms the order is delivered (i.e., a contradiction between tooling and order state), do not repeatedly retry the same tooling call; instead offer escalation and call transfer_to_human_agents({"summary": "Tooling reported non‑deliverable state but order details show delivered"}) to resolve the inconsistency. Include 'last_user_message' in the transfer payload only if the tooling accepts it; if the tooling returns an unexpected-argument error for that field, retry the transfer with only the 'summary' field. Document the inconsistency and proceed with escalation if transfer succeeds.

IF a user requests a refund to a different payment method but the original payment method for the order is a gift card → THEN explicitly inform the user that refunds must be returned to the original gift card, explain why the card refund is required, and offer alternative options (for example: a) initiate a full-order return so the entire order's refund is processed back to the gift card, b) escalate to a human agent if the user insists on an exception). Do not attempt to process the refund to another on-file payment method when the original payment was a gift card. If the user agrees to proceed with a return, call return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id) with the gift card payment_method_id and confirm that follow-up return instructions will be emailed.

acti-014: IF a transfer_to_human_agents tooling call fails with an error indicating an unexpected or unsupported parameter (for example, 'unexpected keyword argument "last_user_message"') → THEN retry the transfer_to_human_agents call with a reduced payload that contains only the required/accepted fields (at minimum, {"summary": <short summary>}). If the retry succeeds, continue the escalation flow and mark the conversation as escalated. If the retry fails for other reasons, follow existing tooling-failure guidance (offer manual escalation, inform the user, and escalate to a human agent if requested). Record the tooling error and the retry attempt in the conversation log.

IF the user requests that refunds/price differences be applied to 'another on-file payment method' but does not specify which one → THEN first retrieve and present the user's available on-file payment methods (prefer non-gift-card methods when the original payment was not a gift card) including identifiable labels (type and last four digits or other label) and indicate which methods the tooling supports for the requested action. Additionally, when a requested action will create a price difference (either a charge or a refund), explicitly show the calculated price difference amount and clearly state whether the user will be charged or refunded, and which on-file payment method (type and last four or other identifiable label) will be used for that charge or refund. Prompt the user to explicitly select and confirm which on-file payment method to use and obtain explicit confirmation of the exact amount and payment method before proceeding. Only after the user confirms a specific payment method and the exact price difference, proceed with the order-changing action using that payment_method_id. If the original payment was a gift card, prefer non‑gift-card methods only when supported by tooling and policy; if tooling does not support changing the refund/payment destination from the original gift card, inform the user and obtain explicit confirmation to proceed with the system behavior. IF no suitable alternate payment method is available, inform the user and offer alternatives (for example: add a new payment method, use a different on-file method they confirm, initiate a return flow if appropriate, or escalate to a human agent). BEFORE executing any irreversible order-changing action, inform the user which on-file payment method (type and last four digits or other identifiable label) will be used and obtain explicit confirmation.
